rulings.Case Title: RODNEY JACKSON II VS. BAUER'S INTELLIGENT TRANSPORTATION, INC.
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss Defendant/Appellants Appeal Of The Order, Decision Or Award And To Enter Judgment Against Defendant/Appellant
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, November 6, 2024, Line 1, PLAINTIFF RODNEY JACKSON's Motion To Dismiss Defendant/Appellants Appeal Of The Order, Decision Or Award And To Enter Judgment Against Defendant/Appellant. Continued to November 8, 2024, to be heard by Judge East. For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/RBU).

rulings.Case Title: JANE DOE VS. SAN FRANCISCO UNIFIED SCHOOL DISTRICT ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, November 6, 2024, Line 8, DEFENDANT SAN FRANCISCO UNIFIED SCHOOL DISTRICT's DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT. Defendant's demurrer to the first amended complaint ("FAC") is overruled as to causes of action one and two and sustained without leave to amend as to cause of action five. Defendant's argument that plaintiff failed to file a government claim within the six-month statute of limitations of Government Code section 911.2(a) lacks merit. The FAC alleges that the claim was presented on July 12, 2023. (FAC, par. 27.) It further alleges that sexual misconduct occurred through February of 2023. (FAC, 33-36.) In addition, the continuing violations doctrine is well established in harassment cases and allows the plaintiff to recover for unlawful practices occurring outside the limitations period. (Richards v. CH2M Hill, Inc. (2001) 26 Cal.4th 798, 823.) The court need not address the CCP 340.1 issue. The government claim states sufficient facts to support the negligent hiring, supervision, retention, and training cause of action. The claim states, inter alia, that defendant's employees did not intervene on behalf of plaintiff after Mr. Doherty's alleged harassment was reported. A government claim need not plead every single evidentiary fact nor does it need to mirror the complaint. "The purpose of the claims statutes isto provide the public entity sufficient information to enable it to adequately investigate claims and to settle them, if appropriate, without the expense of litigation." (City of Stockton v. Superior Court (2007) 42 Cal.4th 730, 738 (internal quotations omitted).) The demurrer to cause of action five for violation of Civil Code 51.9 is sustained without leave to amend. Pursuant to K.M. v. Grossmont Union High School Dist. (2022) 84 Cal.App.5th 717, 756, plaintiff's theories of vicarious liability or ratification fail. For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/RBU).

rulings.Case Title: JANE DOE VS. SAN FRANCISCO UNIFIED SCHOOL DISTRICT ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE Amended COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, November 6, 2024, Line 8, DEFENDANT SAN FRANCISCO UNIFIED SCHOOL DISTRICT's MOTION TO STRIKE Amended COMPLAINT. Defendant's motion to strike is denied. The factual circumstances described in the government claim support the claims in the first amended complaint ("FAC"). "The purpose of the claims statutes isto provide the public entity sufficient information to enable it to adequately investigate claims and to settle them, if appropriate, without the expense of litigation." (City of Stockton v. Superior Court (2007) 42 Cal.4th 730, 738 (internal quotations omitted).) The court rejects defendant's argument that plaintiff's description of events in the government claim (unwanted touching of plaintiff's bottom, stomach and thighs) is markedly different from her allegation of "sexual assault." Plaintiff also pleads sufficient facts to support the relief requested in paragraphs 62 and 96 of the FAC. For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. rulings.Case Title: GEOFFREY LYNCH VS. WELLS FARGO BANK, N.A. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEFENDANT WELLS FARGO FARGO BANK, N.A.'S MOTION TO STRIKE THE SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, November 6, 2024, Line 17, DEFENDANT WELLS FARGO FARGO BANK, N.A.'S MOTION TO STRIKE THE SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT. (Full tentative ruling emailed to counsel). Defendant Wells Fargo Bank, N.A.'s demurrers to causes of action 6 [trespass], 7[wrongful eviction], 8 [conversion] and 9 [intentional infliction of emotional distress] are sustained without leave to amend. These claims are predicated on plaintiff's allegations that while he resided at the premises, defendant illegally locked him out on February 5, 2020. (Second Amended Complaint ("SAC") pars. 29, 50, 53, 135, 137, 153, 173.) The claims are time barred because plaintiff filed this action on April 5, 2024. The statute of limitations for trespass and conversion is three years. (CCP 338.) The statute of limitations for the intentional infliction of emotional distress is two years. (CCP 335.1.) The statute of limitations for wrongful eviction is one year. (Sylve v. Riley (1993) 15 Cal.App.4th 23.). For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/RBU).

Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/RBU).

rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CASE(S) CGC-24-614871 WITH CASE CGC-24-614415
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, November 6, 2024, Line 13, DEFENDANT CRUISE LLC's MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CASE(S) CGC-24-614871 WITH CASE CGC-24-614415. Cruise LLC's motion to consolidate case number 614871 with 614415 is granted; the latter is designated the lead case. The two cases involve the same traffic accident.
